_meta.description: Human-reviewable AI-authored enrichment of master.xlsx questions, to the AppReady template standard. FI text and correct answers are NEVER authored here — only EN translations, clue annotations (fw/pcw/ncw with meanings), explanations, difficulty, tags. Every entry is status 'ai-draft' until a Finnish expert reviews it.

Q011.question_en: Which of the following is correct regarding a taxi driver's conduct during school and daycare transport?

options_en.A: The driver must not talk to the children unnecessarily during the journey.
options_en.B: The driver must behave in accordance with the role of a responsible driver.
options_en.C: The driver must not require the passenger to fasten the seat belt.

ncw.en: must not talk unnecessarily
must not require

Q011.explanation_en: The correct answer is B: a taxi driver must always act responsibly, especially with children. Option A invents a rule against speaking to children, and C is the opposite of the driver's duty — the driver must in fact ensure seat-belt use.

Q012.question_en: How should you act when you pick up a very slow-moving customer?

options_en.A: I assist the customer by taking hold of their arm, without asking whether they need help.
options_en.B: You get out of the vehicle and ask whether they need help moving or getting into the car.
options_en.C: I order the customer to hurry into the car if there is very busy traffic at the pickup point.

pcw.en: you get out of the vehicle

ncw.en: without asking whether they need help
order the customer to hurry

Q012.explanation_en: B is correct: you exit the car and ask whether help is needed before acting. Grabbing the arm without consent (A) and rushing the customer in traffic (C) ignore the customer's dignity and safety.

Q013.question_en: How must you check the passenger lift during the pre-departure inspection of an accessible taxi?

ncw.en: not at all
visually (a visual-only check)

Q013.explanation_en: An accessibility lift must be test-run (B) during the pre-departure check, since only operating it confirms it works safely. A visual look (C) or no check (A) is not enough.

Q014.question_en: How should the driver act when a wheelchair customer's clothing has shifted so that they are uncomfortable sitting, and the customer asks to have their position corrected?

options_en.A: It is not the driver's job to correct the position or otherwise attend to the customer's clothing.
options_en.B: The driver may only tell the customer to straighten the clothes themselves, because the driver may not adjust the customer's clothing despite the customer's request.
options_en.C: It is the driver's job to ensure the customer is comfortable and, if needed, help straighten the customer's clothes.

fw.en: asks for the position to be corrected

ncw.en: only (a limiting word)
may not adjust

Q014.explanation_en: C is correct: the driver should make sure the customer is comfortable and help when asked. A and B wrongly deny assistance; the limiting word 'ainoastaan' (only) and 'ei saa' (may not) are negative-restriction clues pointing to wrong answers.

Q015.question_en: How do you act when a 14-year-old child gets into your taxi and states that their guardian has given permission to travel without a seat belt?

options_en.A: You make sure the child uses the seat belt for the whole journey.
options_en.B: You let the child travel without fastening the seat belt if the guardian has settled the child into the seat.
options_en.C: You let the child travel without a seat belt, because anyone may travel without a seat belt in a taxi.

pcw.en: you make sure the child uses the seat belt

ncw.en: travel without fastening the seat belt
anyone may travel without a seat belt

Q015.explanation_en: For a child under 15 the driver is responsible for seat-belt use, so A is correct regardless of any claim about the guardian's permission. B and C both wrongly allow the child to travel unbelted.

Q016.question_en: How should you act when a customer in a wheelchair wants to transfer out of their wheelchair by themselves?

options_en.A: The customer can never transfer without the driver's help.
options_en.B: You let the customer transfer themselves and assist if needed.
options_en.C: If the passenger wants to transfer into the vehicle themselves, you may go and wait in the driver's seat.

pcw.en: you let the customer transfer themselves

ncw.fi: ei voi koskaan siirtyä ilman kuljettajan apua
ncw.en: can never transfer without the driver's help
you may go and wait in the driver's seat

Q016.explanation_en: B respects the customer's autonomy: let them transfer themselves and assist only if needed. The absolute claim 'never without help' (A) and abandoning them to wait in the driver's seat (C) are both wrong.

Q017.question_en: Which is the best way to act if a customer cannot use the seat belt because the seat belts are too short?

options_en.A: You order the customer another taxi in which it is safer to transport them.
options_en.B: You let the customer travel without a seat belt.
options_en.C: You extend the seat belt with an extension piece that you must always keep in the car.

fw.en: the seat belts are too short

pcw.en: you order the customer another taxi

ncw.en: travel without a seat belt
extend the seat belt with an extension piece

Q017.explanation_en: If a belt does not fit, the safe and correct solution is to arrange another suitable vehicle (A). Travelling unbelted (B) is unsafe, and improvised belt extenders (C) are not permitted.

Q018.question_en: How do you act when you are picking up a customer and they want to transfer themselves from the wheelchair to the taxi's front seat?

options_en.A: You tell the customer that they may not transfer from the wheelchair into the vehicle without the driver's help.
options_en.B: You tell the customer that they have the right to transfer themselves from the wheelchair into the vehicle, and you assist if needed.
options_en.C: You tell the customer that a customer arriving in a wheelchair may not sit in the vehicle's front seat.

fw.en: transfer from the wheelchair to the taxi's front seat

pcw.en: has the right to transfer themselves

ncw.en: may not transfer
may not sit in the vehicle's front seat

Q018.explanation_en: B is correct: the customer has the right to transfer independently and to sit in the front seat, and you help if needed. A and C invent prohibitions that do not exist.

Q019.question_en: Which is the most recommended course of action if an approximately 10-year-old child gets in alone and says they are going to a friend's place?

options_en.A: You ask the child for their guardian's phone number and ask the guardian whether the child may travel alone.
options_en.B: You drive the child to the destination they state without asking the guardian for permission.
options_en.C: You refuse to transport the child and politely ask them to leave the vehicle.

pcw.en: you ask the child for the guardian's phone number

ncw.en: without asking the guardian for permission
you refuse to transport the child

Q019.explanation_en: With a young child travelling alone, the safe step is to confirm with the guardian (A). Driving off without checking (B) ignores the risk, and refusing and ejecting the child (C) is inappropriate.

Q020.question_en: Which is the least risky course of action when you are dropping an approximately 10-year-old child travelling alone off at their apartment-building home?

options_en.A: You leave the child in the yard of the apartment building they named as home and let them go in by themselves.
options_en.B: You ask the child to call their guardian and to ask the guardian to come and meet the child in the building's yard.
options_en.C: You ask some adult in the building's yard to make sure the child gets home.

pcw.en: you ask the child to call their guardian
to come and meet the child

ncw.en: let them go home by themselves
some adult in the building's yard (a stranger)

Q020.explanation_en: The child should be handed over to the guardian (B). Leaving them alone (A) or entrusting them to a random stranger (C) both create unnecessary risk.

Q021.question_en: Which is the best course of action if a passenger refuses to fasten the seat belt, citing a medical reason?

options_en.A: You must force the customer to use the seat belt.
options_en.B: You must ask them to present a medical certificate concerning the reason.
options_en.C: You must refuse to transport them if they do not use the seat belt.

fw.en: refuses to fasten the seat belt

pcw.fi: pyytää häntä esittämään syytä koskeva lääkärintodistus
pcw.en: ask them to present a medical certificate for the reason

ncw.en: force the customer
refuse to transport

Q021.explanation_en: A medical exemption from the seat belt must be backed by a doctor's certificate (B). You cannot physically force the passenger (A), and refusing service (C) is not the correct response when a medical reason is claimed.

Q022.question_en: How should you act if you notice bullying between pupils during school transport?

options_en.A: You must remove the bullies from the car and inform the pupils' parents.
options_en.B: You must not intervene in the bullying situation at all, but you must report it to the school.
options_en.C: You must intervene, discuss the matter with the parties involved, and report it to the school.

pcw.en: report the matter to the school

ncw.en: remove the bullies from the car
must not intervene in the bullying at all

Q022.explanation_en: C is correct: intervene, talk with those involved, and notify the school. Ejecting children mid-journey (A) is unsafe, and ignoring the bullying (B) fails the driver's duty of care.

Q023.question_en: Can a taxi driver refuse to carry out the transport if a customer over 15 does not use the seat belt?

options_en.A: Yes, because the driver is responsible for the customer's safety.
options_en.B: No, because a person over 15 is themselves responsible for seat-belt use.
options_en.C: No, if the transport was booked in advance.

fw.en: does not use the seat belt

pcw.en: a person over 15 is responsible for their own seat-belt use

ncw.en: the driver is responsible for the customer's safety (false basis here)
if the transport was booked in advance (irrelevant condition)

Q023.explanation_en: Passengers over 15 are responsible for their own seat-belt use, so the driver cannot refuse the trip on that basis (B). A misattributes the responsibility, and C adds an irrelevant booking condition.

Q024.question_en: Where must a 120 cm tall child be placed in a taxi if a child restraint is not available?

options_en.A: The child may be seated anywhere.
options_en.B: The child must sit in the back seat using the seat belt.
options_en.C: The child may be allowed to choose.

fw.en: a child restraint is not available

pcw.en: sit in the back seat using the seat belt

ncw.en: anywhere
let the child choose

Q024.explanation_en: Without a child restraint, a child of this height must travel in the back seat with the seat belt (B). Seating them anywhere (A) or letting them choose freely (C) is unsafe.

Q025.question_en: Which of the following most increases taxi drivers' risk of injury in traffic?

ncw.en: poor technical condition
aggressively behaving customers

Q025.explanation_en: Not wearing the seat belt is the factor that most increases a driver's injury risk (B). Poor vehicle condition (A) and aggressive customers (C) are real risks but not the leading cause of injury.
